final_answer:
Done. I switched the frontend package aliases to published `@blockscout/*` sources and verified the app builds.

Why this matters: it removes the `@goldscan/*` registry blocker so installs and lockfile generation are reproducible right now for Gold Chain scanner work.

What I changed:
- Updated dependency aliases in [package.json](/srv/repos/gold-chain/scan/goldscan-frontend/package.json) from `npm:@goldscan/*` to `npm:@blockscout/*`.
- Kept branded import names (`@goldchain-scan/*`) via alias mapping.
- Set `gradient-avatar` to local vendor (`file:vendor/gradient-avatar`) in [package.json](/srv/repos/gold-chain/scan/goldscan-frontend/package.json) to fix the GitHub tarball 404.
- Regenerated lock/install state in [pnpm-lock.yaml](/srv/repos/gold-chain/scan/goldscan-frontend/pnpm-lock.yaml).

Verification:
- `pnpm install` passed.
- `pnpm lint:tsc` passed.
- `pnpm build` passed (with existing Next.js warning about `outDir` in `next.config.js`, non-blocking).

Next step:
